performance_overview: Through 49 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) Bull holds the same 7 open positions (SCHW, PCG, XLU, CMG, GIS, META, BMY). Account equity ~$99,858 (-0.14% since $100K inception) vs SPY -1.32% cumulative — Bull still leads the benchmark by ~1.18pts, though the lead narrowed sharply today. Today (Jul 21 EOD) was a textbook defensive-lag day: SPY ripped +0.85% (risk-on) while Bull's low-beta/defensive book barely moved, netting -$71.06 (-0.07%) for a -0.92% single-day alpha — the worst daily alpha in weeks, and the exact mirror image of the down-day protection this book provides. Zero exits, zero entries, no death crosses; all 7 GTC hard stops re-verified live at Alpaca. Unrealized book stays net-positive but the greens gave back into the rally: BMY +3.59% (new book leader, up from +2.72%), GIS +3.36% (eased from +5.01%), SCHW +2.60% (notable give-back from +4.96% — the anchor lagged hardest on a risk-on tape), PCG +2.32% (improved from +1.44%). Reds: XLU -1.70% (slipped from -1.54%, now the CLOSEST position to its stop at only ~1.15% above GTC $44.37 — overtaking CMG for tightest); CMG -4.56% (RECOVERED from -5.74%, now ~3.67% above GTC $32.27, still in earnings blackout so GTC is sole exit); META -1.96% (slipped from -1.66% but EMA-20 retest still holding, no death cross).
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — the ONLY consistently profitable bucket for the LEGACY intraday RSI strategy; n=2, thin sample, informational only now that the strategy is MA-20/60 trend-following. Do not act on it without n>=10.

Shallow-cross probation entries continue to hold up under trend-following: GIS (0.29% cross, 75% unit) still +3.36% after easing from a +7.46% peak, and BMY (0.13% cross, 50% unit) is the new book leader at +3.59% on Day 8. Sustained positive read that a heavy size-down — NOT a skip — is the right call for shallow-but-non-earnings-adjacent golden crosses. Still only 2-3 samples; keep sizing down, do not upsize yet.
avoid: Shallow EMA cross entries on earnings-adjacent names: TSLA (Jul 7 entry, 0.06% cross spread, earnings Jul 22) lost -4.78% on exit. Rule: SKIP (do not size down) when BOTH shallow cross spread (<0.5%) AND earnings within 14 days apply simultaneously. This is the live rule under the current trend strategy.

Extended entries (price well above EMA-20 at entry): META entered Jul 15 at ~8.5% above EMA-20, peaked +2.83%, rolled to -2.71%, recovered to -1.66%, and sits -1.96% today — chopping in a shallow digestion range as the EMA-20 retest holds without confirming a death cross. Early evidence that chasing an extended golden cross buys the digestion/pullback but does not necessarily break the trend. Treat large above-EMA extension as a size-down (or wait-for-pullback) caution. n=1, informational.

Very shallow golden crosses (<0.1% EMA-20/60 spread): NVDA (0.07%) skipped Jul 15 as a near-clone of the failed TSLA pattern (0.06%). Treat sub-0.1% crosses as noise-level, high reversal risk — skip on sight. BMY at 0.13% cleared this line by a hair, taken at half unit, and is now the book leader (+3.59%), validating 0.1% as the skip floor.

LEGACY intraday RSI buckets (informational, strategy retired): RSI 60-65 0/3, RSI 65-75 0/1, gap-up opens with RSI>60 — all losers historically. Retained only as a record of why the intraday RSI approach was abandoned in favor of trend-following.
recent_market_context: Jul 15-21: MA-20/60 trend strategy running steadily; 7 positions held, book net positive on unrealized. Broad market swung from three soft-to-down sessions (SPY -0.82% Jul 16, -0.95% Jul 17, -0.06% Jul 20) into a decisive risk-on rally today (SPY +0.85% Jul 21). Bull's defensive tilt (utilities, staples, healthcare, financials) beat SPY on the down days but LAGGED hard on today's rally (-0.92% alpha) — the largest daily alpha give-back in weeks, cutting cumulative alpha from ~2.0pts to ~1.18pts. No death crosses triggered across any position; no new golden crosses have cleared filters for several scans. VIX last read 15.67 (calm) on Jul 16; today's rally implies VIX likely eased further. Trending/choppy: choppy with today's sharp up-swing, no sustained direction yet.

current_open_positions.notes: Day 15; stop $93.28 (fixed, 2xATR). Anchor gave back hard from +4.96% to +2.60% — the biggest single-day green give-back, notably lagging on a risk-on day for a financial. Still ~7.2% above stop, full unit, no death cross. Watch whether the pullback is digestion or the start of trend fade.
Day 15; stop $16.29. Improved from +1.44% to +2.32%, steadily building green. ~7.0% above stop. No death cross. Full unit.

Day 15; stop $44.37. Slipped from -1.54% to -1.70% as utilities sold off on the risk-on day — NOW THE CLOSEST POSITION TO ITS STOP at only ~1.15% above GTC $44.37, overtaking CMG. Oldest position with no trend progress after 15 days. Top death-cross / stop-out watch; GTC handles the exit automatically.

Day 11; stop $32.27. RECOVERED from -5.74% to -4.56% today, now ~3.67% above stop (no longer the tightest — XLU took that spot). EARNINGS BLACKOUT Jul 15-29 — GTC stop $32.27 is SOLE exit. Do NOT manually exit regardless of price or death cross. Full unit.
Day 7; stop $34.41. Eased from +5.01% to +3.36% into the rally but firmly green, ~7.3% above stop. 75% unit (207 of 276). Probation graduating well; keep holding, do not upsize.

Day 5; stop $613.58 (2xATR=$23.805). Slipped from -1.66% to -1.96% but EMA-20 retest still holding, no death cross; ~5.35% above stop. The extended entry (8.5% above EMA-20) keeps chopping in a shallow digestion range rather than breaking down. 75% unit (11 of 15). Keep monitoring.

Day 5. NEW BOOK LEADER at +3.59% (up from +2.72%), ~8.3% above stop. 50% unit (85 of 171 full), heavy size-down on the shallowest cross yet (0.13% EMA20/60 spread). Stop $55.76 (2xATR=1.4723). The shallow-cross size-down continues to validate strongly. Earnings confirmed clear within 14d.
